site.title: Loomis Docs
site.description: A calm, editorial documentation starter for guides, references, and changelogs.

footer.copyright_text: © 2026 Loomis Press. Set in Lora and Outfit.

pages.title: Installation
pages.excerpt: Get a local copy running in under a minute. The starter has no required configuration.
pages.content:
Requirements

You'll need Node 18 or newer and any package manager you like — npm, pnpm, or bun all work. No database, no environment variables, no accounts.

Install

Clone the project and install dependencies. The starter uses Vite, React, and Tailwind, so the install is small and fast.

terminal
# clone & install
git clone https://example.com/loomis-docs.git
cd loomis-docs
npm install

Run locally

Start the dev server with npm run dev. The site reloads as you edit any page.

i

Hot tip

Pages are plain React components — duplicate Installation.tsx and rename it to bootstrap a new doc in seconds.

pages.title: Project Structure
pages.excerpt: Three folders, one design system, no surprises.
pages.content:
Folder tree

The starter keeps things flat on purpose. You should be able to find any file by guessing.

project
src/
  components/docs/   # layout, sidebar, prose, TOC
  pages/docs/        # one file per guide
  index.css          # the entire design system

Where to add things

New guide? Drop a file in pages/docs/ and add it to docsContent.ts. New visual primitive? Add it to Prose.tsx so every page can reuse it.

i

One source of truth

Sidebar, search, and TOC all read from the same content index — change it once, change it everywhere.

pages.title: Writing Content
pages.content:
Voice

Write the way you'd explain something to a colleague at a coffee shop. Friendly, specific, never condescending. If you wouldn't say it out loud, don't put it on the page.

Structure

Each page should answer one question. Use H2 headings for the steps, and keep paragraphs to three or four sentences. Long pages tire readers; split them.

Callouts

Three flavors only — note, tip, warning. Reach for them sparingly; if every paragraph is a callout, none of them are.

!

Don't overdo it

Two callouts per page is plenty. Three is a smell.

pages.title: Code Examples
pages.excerpt: Warm, dark code blocks with lightweight token highlighting.
pages.content:
Basic block

Wrap any block of code in the .code-block shell. The chrome above the snippet is decorative; the copy button appears on hover.

app.ts
import { createApp } from "loomis";

const app = createApp({ name: "docs" });
app.listen(3000);

Syntax highlighting

Highlighting is just CSS classes — no client-side parser required. Wrap tokens in .tok-keyword, .tok-fn, .tok-string, or .tok-comment to color them.

Inline code

Use backticks for short snippets inside a sentence. They render with a warm chalk background and the terracotta accent.

pages.title: Theming
pages.excerpt: HSL tokens, swatches, and how to rebrand.
pages.content:
Tokens

Every color is an HSL token in assets/style.css. Light and dark themes share the same names — only the values change under html.dark.

style.css
:root {
  --canvas: hsl(45 22% 93%);
  --ink-dark: hsl(18 8% 21%);
  --tab-terracotta: hsl(11 43% 58%);
}

Swatches

The palette has three accents — terracotta, sage, and ochre — used for the three sidebar groups, the three callout flavors, and the link/active states.

Customizing

To rebrand, change the three --tab-* tokens at the top of style.css. Everything that uses them — sidebar accents, callouts, links — updates in one move.

i

Stay in HSL

HSL makes it easy to dial saturation and lightness without rebuilding the palette from scratch.

pages.title: Components
pages.excerpt: Reference for the building blocks that ship with the starter.
pages.content:
DocsLayout

The page shell. Renders the sidebar, header (category + title + intro), prose area, optional table of contents, and prev/next pagination.

props
type Props = {
  category: string;
  chapter?: string;
  title: string;
  intro: string;
  toc?: TocItem[];
}

Prose primitives

Use P, H2, H3, and InlineCode instead of raw HTML so the editorial styling stays consistent. Headings accept an id so the table of contents can scroll to them.

Callout

Three variants: note, tip, and warning. Each maps to one of the palette accents.

i

Composition matters

These primitives are intentionally tiny — they're meant to be copied, extended, and remixed for your own voice.

pages.title: Changelog
pages.excerpt: Every release, what changed, and why.
pages.content:
v1.1.0 — May 2026

  • Mobile-friendly sidebar with overlay drawer.
  • Command-palette search (⌘K) across pages and sections.
  • Deep-paper night mode with a smooth toggle.

v1.0.0 — March 2026

  • First public release.
  • Editorial Mentor palette, Lora + Outfit type system.
  • Sidebar, TOC, callouts, code blocks, pagination.

pages.title: Cookie Policy
pages.excerpt: How Loomis uses cookies on this site.
pages.content:
## What we use

A single preference cookie remembers your dark-mode choice. That's it.

## How to opt out

Clear site data in your browser at any time. The site continues to work without it.

pages.title: Privacy Policy
pages.excerpt: What we collect, why, and your rights.
pages.content:
## Overview

Loomis Docs is a static site. We don't collect personal data.

## Logs

The hosting provider may log standard request metadata (IP, user agent) for abuse prevention. We don't read it.

pages.title: Terms
pages.excerpt: The agreement between you and Loomis.
pages.content:
## Use

Loomis Docs is provided as-is. Use it freely for your own documentation projects.

## License

MIT.
